title: Market was already bracing for conflict
detail: Reuters said Brent had already reached a six-month high as traders worried that military action against Iran was getting closer.

title: Current war begins
detail: Reuters said U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran triggered the current conflict window used in this page.

title: Shipping and oil react immediately
detail: Reuters said Brent jumped to as high as $82.37 and around 150 ships were stranded as shipping disruption spread through the Gulf.

title: Insurance support is expanded
detail: Reuters said the U.S. moved to reinsure Gulf maritime losses up to about $20 billion after tanker transit had ground to a halt.

title: War looked predictable to specialist risk desks
detail: Reuters reported that advisory firms were assigning high probabilities to military action before the strikes actually happened.

title: Hormuz traffic collapse becomes measurable
detail: Reuters said traffic through the Strait of Hormuz had dropped 97% since the war began.

title: Energy facilities are hit and oil spikes again
detail: Reuters said Brent settled at $107.38 after strikes on major Middle East energy assets and kept rising after settlement.

title: Latest accessible Archean market read
detail: Archean's accessible historical price series had recovered above its pre-war close by March 24, but the company still had not reported a post-war quarter.
